description: Event-to-subagent dispatch matrix for the heartbeat orchestrator. Each entry binds a GitHub PR event shape to one subagent in .claude/agents/. The heartbeat consults this file when a webhook (or a polling scan) observes the event.

events.rationale: A new (non-draft) PR adding an outcome+report pair should get a structured rubric review. Posts one comment in the documented 4-section format.
events.dedup: skip if any prior alex-jadecli comment on this PR starts with '## outcome-reviewer'

events.rationale: A red CI check on outcome-gate or typecheck means the PR cannot merge until classified. The firefighter posts a triage comment and tags the author.
events.dedup: skip if any prior alex-jadecli comment on this PR contains 'ci-firefighter' AND the failure classification is unchanged

events.rationale: When scripts/eval.sh emits a fresh evals/results/<date>.json, the tightener inspects the confusion matrix and proposes one ports-stable rubric edit (or returns no_tightening_needed if the matrix is error-free).
events.dedup: skip if a runroc at outcomes/runrocs/E3-rubric-tightener.md already exists for the same matrix date AND the verdict is no_tightening_needed

fallback.rationale: Unknown events are ignored; the heartbeat continues with the next QUEUE item.

notes.invocation_surface: Today: heartbeat reads QUEUE.md, scans open PRs via 'gh pr list', and dispatches the matching subagent via the Agent tool. Future: a GitHub Actions workflow could POST webhook events to a Worker that calls the heartbeat directly.
notes.dedup_policy_owner: each subagent enforces its own dedup per its role spec; this file documents the expected behavior but is not the source of truth
